title: My Demo Project
description: This project defines a DataWeave library, `example-project`, designed to encapsulate reusable transformation logic. Its primary function, as seen in `main.dwl`, is to perform a simple identity transformation on an input payload, converting it to JSON format. This library is intended to be consumed by other Mule applications, providing a standardized and shareable piece of DataWeave code without containing any direct Mule flows or connectors. It promotes modularity by centralizing common data transformation patterns.

bestPractices.title: Hardcoded Secrets
bestPractices.reason: No hardcoded credentials, tokens, or sensitive URLs were found within the provided DataWeave script or POM file. The POM mentions external credential management (e.g., in ~/.m2/settings.xml) for publishing to Exchange.

bestPractices.title: Global Error Handler
bestPractices.reason: As this is a DataWeave library (packaging dw-library), it does not contain Mule flows or global error handlers. Error handling is the responsibility of the consuming Mule application, not the library itself.

bestPractices.title: Secure Property Placeholders
bestPractices.reason: Secure property placeholders (e.g., secure::) are not applicable within a DataWeave library project. Property management, including secure properties, is handled by the consuming Mule application.

bestPractices.title: Clear and Concise DataWeave Logic
bestPractices.reason: The DataWeave script `main.dwl` is very simple and clear, performing a straightforward identity transformation on the payload to JSON, which makes its purpose immediately understandable.

documentation:
# Project Guide

## Overview
This project, `example-project`, is a DataWeave library containing reusable transformation logic. It is built using Maven with `dw-library` packaging. The core functionality is defined in `src/main/dw/main.dwl`, which processes an incoming payload and outputs it as JSON. This library can be imported and utilized by any Mule 4 application to apply the defined transformations.

## DataWeave Scripts
- **`main.dwl`**: The main transformation script. It performs an identity mapping on the input `payload`, converting the result to `application/json`.

## Dependencies
Key dependencies include the DataWeave runtime and core modules, enabling robust data transformation capabilities. Testing dependencies are also included for unit testing the DataWeave scripts.

## Usage
To use this library, add it as a dependency in your Mule application's `pom.xml`. Once imported, you can invoke the DataWeave functions defined within this library from your Mule flows to transform data.

cards.title: Payload Identity Transformation
cards.description: The `main.dwl` script processes the incoming `payload`. It iterates over the items within the payload (assuming an array or iterable structure) and returns each item without modification. The entire output is formatted as `application/json`. This acts as a pass-through transformation for the payload, ensuring it's in the desired JSON format.
